description: ClawLoop is an AI Agent Reinforcement Learning framework that continuously optimizes agent behavior through a dual-path learning architecture. It combines "Soft Updates"—prompt engineering and playbook curation—with "Hard Updates"—weight fine‑tuning, operating in a feedback loop where interaction data is collected via environment simulation or a transparent proxy, transformed into rewarded episodes, and then used to update the agent's internal layers (Harness, Router, Weights) via a two‑phase commit protocol (Accumulate‑Apply). The system orchestrates this loop, manages state, and integrates various components for environment interaction, data processing, observation, and optimization.

components.name: Agent Orchestrator
components.description: The central management unit that coordinates the high‑level execution loop, manages agent state, and enforces the Layer protocol for learning updates. It serves as the primary entry point for both CLI and Server‑based operations.

components.name: Core Orchestration Engine
components.description: The primary execution driver that manages the agent's cognitive state and the iterative learning loop, monitoring performance signals via adaptive intensity logic to trigger reflections or state updates.

components.name: Cognitive Strategy Controller
components.description: Acts as the primary decision-making interface for the agent. It manages the high-level lifecycle, including the ingestion of data and the triggering of learning processes. It utilizes adaptive intensity logic to monitor performance signals (like reward deltas) to decide when the agent should reflect, update its internal models via the AsyncLearner, or undergo a "Paradigm Breakthrough."

components.name: Loop Execution Engine
components.description: Implements the iterative "Learning Loop" that drives the agent through specific episodes. It is responsible for maintaining the AgentState (a snapshot of cognitive and weight states) and recording every iteration's outcome. It provides the telemetry necessary to track the agent's evolution over time, identifying trends in performance that inform the strategy controller.

components_relations.relation: Triggers iterative episode execution and provides the target cognitive parameters for the loop.
Returns performance telemetry and reward signals used to calculate adaptive intensity and trigger reflections.

components.name: System Access & Lifecycle
components.description: Manages external entry points and the background lifecycle of the agent, handling transitions from user commands (CLI/API) to active learning processes, and overseeing asynchronous execution and server‑side event broadcasting.

components.name: External Access & Command Interface
components.description: This component acts as the primary entry point for the system. It handles CLI command parsing, manages the FastAPI-based server infrastructure, and enforces security through authentication middleware. It is responsible for ingesting external data, sanitizing it via LLM clients, and routing control signals to the underlying learning engine.

components.name: Learning Lifecycle Orchestrator
components.description: Manages the background "active learning" phase of the agent. It centers on the AsyncLearner, which reacts to data ingestion events by triggering optimization cycles. This component coordinates the transition between data accumulation and model evolution, utilizing specific backends to perform weight or prompt updates without interrupting the main server flow.

components_relations.relation: Dispatches lifecycle control signals (start/stop/reset) and triggers learning cycles by passing ingested data batches to the background learner
Broadcasts asynchronous event updates, learning metrics, and optimization progress back to the server for real-time monitoring and client notification

components.name: Learning Protocol Implementation
components.description: Enforces the Layer protocol that decouples state mutation from gradient/signal accumulation, providing forward_backward and optim_step interfaces for updating agent weights and prompts.

components.name: Learning Protocol Definition
components.description: Establishes the fundamental contract for all learning components in the framework. It defines the Layer protocol which mandates the separation of sampling, signal accumulation, and optimization steps, ensuring a consistent interface for high‑level agents to interact with diverse learning implementations.

components.name: Weight-Based Learning Implementation
components.description: A concrete implementation of the Layer protocol specialized for managing neural network weights and agent parameters. It orchestrates the transition from sampling to gradient accumulation (using mechanisms like GRPO) and manages internal "pending" states to ensure updates are only applied during the authorized optimization phase.

components.name: Backend Execution Abstraction
components.description: Provides a low‑level interface for weight operations, abstracting the specific compute engine or RL library (e.g., SkyRL, Tinker) used for the actual mathematical optimization and data persistence. It handles the physical saving/loading of model states and the execution of the optimization algorithm.

delegates to

components.name: Environment & Backend Abstraction
components.description: Provides a unified interface for interacting with diverse task environments and reinforcement learning backends, abstracting episode sampling and low‑level weight updates.

components.name: Weight Management & Optimization Backend
components.description: Defines the core protocols for managing agent weights and the data structures for learning signals. It implements the "Two-Phase Commit" pattern (Accumulate-Apply) to ensure stable updates by decoupling gradient accumulation from state mutation.

components.name: Task Environment & Evaluation
components.description: Provides the abstraction for task discovery, execution, and success measurement. It treats environments as black boxes that return episodes, and utilizes reward extractors to translate raw environment outputs into scalar signals for the learning process.

components.name: Environment & Backend Adapters
components.description: Contains the concrete implementations of the environment and backend interfaces. It acts as the integration layer that maps external system calls (like Docker container management or SkyRL optimization) to the internal framework protocols.

components.name: Learning Loop Orchestrator
components.description: The central engine that drives the reinforcement learning process. It coordinates the interaction between environment adapters and the agent's state, managing versioning and ensuring data flows correctly from sampling to optimization.

components.name: LLM & Reward Processing
components.description: Handles LLM communication and extracts structured rewards from raw model outputs, translating environment outcomes into learning signals.

components.name: LLM Communication Interface
components.description: Provides a provider-agnostic interface for interacting with Large Language Models. It handles the lifecycle of completion requests and encapsulates raw responses into structured `CompletionResult` objects, facilitating easy manipulation and comparison of model outputs across different providers (e.g., via LiteLLM).

components.name: Reward Extraction Engine
components.description: Responsible for the semantic interpretation of agent outcomes and model completions. It parses raw data to determine the success or quality of an agent's action, translating environment-specific results into the numerical or structured reward signals required by the optimization layers (Harness/Evolver).

components.name: Learning & Evolution Engine
components.description: Responsible for the "Soft Update" logic, managing the agent's playbook of insights and evolving prompts through mutation and crossover. It also handles background maintenance tasks like synthetic data generation (dreaming) and playbook consolidation.

components.name: Evolutionary Learning Core (Harness)
components.description: This component serves as the primary orchestrator for the learning protocol, implementing the forward_backward and optim_step interfaces. It manages the lifecycle of prompt evolution, using evolutionary strategies to generate and evaluate prompt candidates based on performance metrics. It also provides the foundational data structures (Episodes) and LLM communication infrastructure required for the agent to interact with models and record experiences.

components.name: Harness Orchestrator
components.description: The central controller of the learning lifecycle. It implements the forward_backward and optim_step interfaces of the learning protocol, maintains the Playbook (a collection of prompt candidates) and uses a ParetoFront to prune underperforming prompts based on metrics like reward and token cost.

components.name: Evolutionary Strategy Engine
components.description: Responsible for the generative adaptation of prompts. It implements genetic algorithms, including mutation and crossover operators, to transform successful prompt templates into new variations, handling the Generative Evolutionary Prompt Adaptation (GEPA) process.

components.name: Paradigm Shift Manager
components.description: Manages high‑level qualitative optimizations known as "Paradigm Breakthroughs." It analyzes long‑term performance to generate structural insights and new conceptual directions for the agent's prompting strategy.

components.name: Experience & Communication Layer
components.description: Provides the foundational data models and LLM infrastructure. It defines the Episode structure, capturing sequences of messages, tool calls, and rewards, and offers a standardized LiteLLMClient for interacting with various model providers.

components_relations.relation: triggers generation of new prompt candidates during optim_step based on current top‑performers
invokes breakthrough logic to generate qualitative insights when incremental evolution reaches a plateau
evaluates fitness of prompt candidates by analyzing recorded Episode data and performance metrics
utilizes LiteLLMClient to perform LLM‑guided mutations and crossovers on prompt strings
analyzes historical Episode sequences to identify patterns and failures that necessitate a paradigm shift

components.name: Knowledge & Reflection Engine
components.description: Responsible for the agent's semantic memory and self-improvement logic. It analyzes completed episodes to extract "Insights"—structured lessons learned from successes and failures. These insights are stored in a Playbook, where they are semantically indexed, deduplicated, and merged by the Curator. This component ensures that the agent's evolving prompts are grounded in a high-quality, curated history of past experiences.

components.name: Experience Representation
components.description: Defines the fundamental data structures for the agent's history. It captures the raw interactions, metadata, and outcomes of tasks, providing the standardized input required for reflection and analysis.

components.name: Reflection Engine
components.description: The active analytical component that processes completed episodes. It utilizes LLMs to perform "offline" reasoning, identifying patterns of success or failure and distilling them into structured, reusable insights.

components.name: Semantic Playbook
components.description: Manages the agent's long-term semantic memory. It stores insights in a Playbook and employs a Curator to perform semantic indexing, merging, and deduplication, ensuring the knowledge base remains concise and relevant.

components.name: Knowledge Orchestrator
components.description: Coordinates the lifecycle of knowledge generation and maintenance. It schedules background tasks for reflection and playbook consolidation, ensuring that the learning loop operates autonomously and efficiently.

components.name: Background Operations & Dreaming
components.description: Manages asynchronous maintenance tasks that optimize the agent's performance outside of active task execution. The Scheduler triggers "Episode Dreaming," where the agent generates synthetic scenarios to explore the state space and refine its strategies. It also orchestrates periodic playbook consolidation to prevent memory bloat and maintain the relevance of stored insights.

components.name: Task Orchestration Engine
components.description: Acts as the central heartbeat of the background subsystem. It manages the lifecycle of all background tasks, tracking elapsed time and episode counts to determine when maintenance is required. It provides a consistent `BackgroundState` snapshot to tasks, ensuring they operate on stable data without interfering with the active agent state.

components.name: Synthetic Dreaming Engine
components.description: Implements the "Episode Dreaming" logic, which performs meta-analysis across multiple execution episodes. It identifies recurring failure modes or successful behavioral patterns that are not apparent during single-step learning. It leverages LLMs to generate synthetic insights, which are then integrated back into the agent's strategic playbook.

components.name: Playbook Maintenance Engine
components.description: Responsible for the long-term health and retrieval efficiency of the agent's knowledge base. It triggers periodic cleanup operations to prevent "memory bloat" by merging semantically similar insights and pruning obsolete or low-value entries. It relies on specialized curation logic to maintain the relevance and quality of the stored playbooks.

components_relations.relation: Orchestrates the execution of episode analysis and pattern generation based on system idle time
Schedules periodic knowledge base pruning and optimization tasks
Generates the strategic insights that are subsequently managed, merged, and consolidated by the maintenance engine

components.name: Environment Integration Layer
components.description: Acts as the concrete bridge between the abstract learning engine and specific task domains. It implements environment-specific agents that consume the instructions and prompt updates provided by the Harness. This layer translates domain-specific actions and observations into the standardized Episode format used by the rest of the engine.

components.name: A2A Protocol & Schema Adapter
components.description: Manages the external communication interface, parsing JSON‑RPC/A2A messages and translating environment‑specific tool schemas into standardized OpenAI‑compatible formats.

components.name: Learning-Guided LLM Orchestrator
components.description: Orchestrates LLM interactions by injecting dynamic prompts from the Harness and normalizing model responses into a consistent internal representation for the agent.

components.name: Task Environment & Evaluation
components.description: Provides the high‑level abstractions for defining tasks and evaluating agent performance. It manages task discovery and generates the rewards/feedback used by the learning layer to optimize behavior.

components_relations.relation: Triggers agent actions by sending domain‑specific JSON‑RPC messages to the agent's communication interface
Forwards parsed messages and translated tool definitions to be processed by the LLM

components.name: Environment & Task Interface
components.description: Provides a standardized abstraction layer for interacting with diverse task domains (e.g., Math, CAR, Harbor). It executes agent actions within these environments to generate raw interaction traces.

components.name: Environment Core Contracts
components.description: Defines the fundamental data structures and abstract interfaces for tasks and evaluation results. It establishes the "language" used by the agent to request work and receive feedback.

components.name: Task Data Models
components.description: Defines the immutable data containers that serve as the "language" of the subsystem. These models represent the static input for tasks and the structured output of evaluations, ensuring type safety across the agent-environment boundary.

components.name: Environment Abstraction Layer
components.description: Provides the abstract behavioral contracts (Protocols) and standard adapters for task management. It defines how the framework requests work units and triggers scoring, allowing the agent to remain agnostic to the specific task domain (e.g., GSM8K vs. HumanEval).

components.name: Feedback & Reward Integration
components.description: Bridges the gap between raw environment outputs and the framework's internal learning state. It maps numeric scores and textual feedback from `EvalResult` into the rewards and episode summaries used by the `Harness` for optimization.

components_relations.relation: Uses `Sample` and `EvalResult` to define the input/output signatures of the `get_tasks` and `evaluate` methods.
Invokes the `evaluate` method on the `TaskEnvironment` protocol to trigger the scoring process.
Consumes and parses `EvalResult` objects to extract numeric scores for reward calculation and textual feedback for prompt optimization.

components.name: Adapter Base Framework
components.description: Implements the Adapter Pattern to provide a reusable foundation for all environment‑specific logic. It manages the lifecycle of environment interactions, including batch execution and episode tracing.

components.name: Environment Lifecycle & Registry
components.description: Manages the initialization of environment-specific resources and the discovery of available tasks. It ensures that the adapter is correctly configured (e.g., establishing connections or preparing containers) and provides the metadata necessary for the agent to select specific tasks or data splits.

components.name: Episode Execution Engine
components.description: The core runtime component responsible for executing agent actions within the environment. It orchestrates the transition from task definitions to completed Episode objects, supporting both single‑episode runs and optimized batch processing to maximize throughput during training or evaluation.

components.name: Evaluation & Trace Provider
components.description: Processes completed episodes to generate performance scores and detailed execution traces. It bridges the gap between raw environment logs and the structured feedback required by the learning layers, enabling the "Collector/Tracer" pattern for agent reflection and optimization.

components_relations.relation: Supplies initialized environment state and task metadata required to begin execution
Forwards completed episode data and raw execution logs for scoring and telemetry extraction

components.name: Concrete Task Environments
components.description: Contains domain‑specific implementations that bridge the framework to external systems. This includes standard reasoning environments (CAR) and specialized proxy layers for adversarial simulations (Purple), handling low‑level details like port management and protocol translation.

components.name: CAR Task Environment
components.description: Implements the core logic for the Cyber Attack/Reasoning (CAR) domain. It manages the execution lifecycle of individual episodes and parallel batches, handling low‑level resource orchestration such as finding free ports to ensure environment isolation. It performs the Adapter role by mapping raw scenario results and execution traces into the framework’s internal Episode data structures.

components.name: Purple Adversarial Proxy
components.description: Provides a specialized proxy layer for adversarial simulations, enabling the framework to interact with external tools via a JSON‑RPC interface. It handles complex protocol translation, such as normalizing assistant messages and reconciling tool call IDs to maintain compatibility with OpenAI‑style APIs. This component allows the framework to treat adversarial simulations as standard environments while managing the underlying network communication.

components.name: Data & Reward Processing
components.description: Transforms raw interaction traces into structured Episode objects and calculates reward signals using LLM‑as‑a‑judge or outcome‑based extractors. This component provides the critical feedback signal for the RL loop.

components.name: Episode Data Model
components.description: Defines the fundamental data structures and schema for agent‑environment interactions. It acts as the primary data contract for the entire subsystem, providing methods to convert traces into standard formats (e.g., OpenAI messages) and calculating basic metrics like effective rewards.

components.name: Core Episode Schema & Metrics
components.description: Defines the structural and analytical foundation of the subsystem. It encapsulates the core data models for interaction traces and provides the logic for calculating performance metrics and serializing data for LLM consumption. This component acts as the "Source of Truth" for the state of any given task execution, providing methods to calculate `effective_reward` and convert traces into OpenAI‑compatible message formats.

components.name: Environment Ingestion Adapters
components.description: Implements the Adapter pattern to bridge external environment execution results into the standardized Episode data model. It handles the parsing and mapping of raw traces from specific task domains (such as Entropic or CAR) into the core schema. This layer ensures that the framework can remain environment‑agnostic by reconciling protocol differences, such as tool‑call formats and metadata structures, before they reach the learning layer.

components.name: Environment Adapters
components.description: Responsible for the "Data Transformation" phase, bridging various task environments (e.g., CAR, Math, Harbor) to the unified Episode format. These adapters execute tasks and map raw execution traces into structured objects that the learning loop can process.

components.name: Core Types & Math Environment Adapter
components.description: Establishes the foundational data schema for the entire framework and provides a reference implementation. It defines the `Future`, `Datum`, and `Episode` types that all other adapters must target, while the `MathAdapter` serves as a lightweight example of mapping direct evaluations to these types.

components.name: Harbor Task Orchestration
components.description: Manages batch execution and orchestration for software‑centric tasks. It utilizes an asynchronous bridge to run tasks in the Harbor environment and contains specialized logic for computing step boundaries and building structured episodes from task results.

components.name: Entropic Trace Mapping
components.description: Handles the complex setup and trace extraction for the Entropic environment. It manages infrastructure concerns like port allocation and health checks, focusing on the "Trace Mapping" process that transforms raw execution logs into the unified learning format.

components.name: CAR Scenario Adapter
components.description: Specializes in scenario‑based simulation management. It generates specific scenarios for the CAR environment and performs the critical transformation of simulation traces into structured episodes, bridging the gap between complex simulation outputs and the agent's learning protocol.

components.name: Reward Extraction Engine
components.description: Implements the logic for calculating feedback signals. It utilizes a pipeline of extractors, ranging from qualitative LLM‑based judges to objective outcome‑based verifiers, to produce a RewardSignal for each episode.

components.name: Reward Pipeline Manager
components.description: Manages the orchestration and conditional execution of the extraction sequence. It iterates through registered extractors to enrich the episode summary with feedback, implementing logic to skip redundant or expensive steps based on the confidence of previously extracted signals.

components.name: Qualitative LLM Judge
components.description: Implements sophisticated, LLM‑driven evaluation of agent behavior. It uses a multi‑vote consensus mechanism to provide qualitative scores and confidence levels, typically acting as a fallback or refinement signal when objective metrics are unavailable.

components.name: Deterministic & Outcome Verifiers
components.description: Combines environment‑specific verification with heuristic‑based extraction. This component interacts with the task environment to validate outcomes (e.g., checking if a file was created or a test passed) and captures immediate execution signals like tool errors or human‑in‑the‑loop feedback.

components.name: Reward Data Model & Formatting
components.description: Defines the standardized schema for feedback signals and provides normalization utilities. It ensures that disparate signals—whether a binary success flag from a unit test or a floating‑point score from an LLM—are formatted consistently for the learning layer.

invokes LLM‑based evaluation as a secondary refinement step if deterministic signals are insufficient

aggregates all extracted signals into the final EpisodeSummary for downstream optimization

components.description: Manages the high‑level flow of data through the subsystem. It orchestrates the "enrichment" of episodes with rewards and aggregates episode‑level metrics into summaries that are consumed by the main RL learning loop.

components.name: Loop Orchestrator
components.description: Drives the high-level reinforcement learning cycle, managing agent state transitions and coordinating the flow between interaction and optimization.

components.name: Observation & Proxy Layer
components.description: Acts as a transparent interceptor for existing agent‑LLM traffic, allowing ClawLoop to collect training data from external systems without code modification.

components.name: Interception & Session Layer
components.description: This component provides the entry points for data collection. It implements a transparent OpenAI-compatible proxy to intercept network traffic and provides library-level hooks (e.g., for LiteLLM) to capture internal agent calls. It is responsible for maintaining session state across stateless HTTP requests and parsing raw LLM responses into structured tool calls and content.

components.name: Interception Gateway & Session Manager
components.description: Provides entry points for capturing agent‑LLM traffic, implements a transparent proxy and internal library hooks, manages session state, and parses raw LLM responses into structured data.

components.name: Episode Collector & Ingestion API
components.description: Acts as the central sink for intercepted data, buffers incoming events, resolves episode boundaries, and provides a REST API for manual ingestion or human feedback, ensuring proper formatting and persistence.

components.name: LLM & Environment Abstraction Layer
components.description: Standardizes LLM output representations and bridges intercepted traffic to task environments, providing unified data structures and adapters that execute episodes to retrieve execution traces and reward signals.

components_relations.relation: Forwards parsed and session‑linked interactions (Episodes) for buffering and storage
Utilizes standardized completion results and client abstractions to normalize intercepted LLM responses

components.name: Central Ingestion & Collection Hub
components.description: Acts as the central sink and management authority for all observed data. It hosts the ingestion API, manages the EpisodeCollector for buffering and deduplicating episodes, and enforces memory eviction policies. It ensures that data from various sources (proxy, hooks, or environment adapters) is unified before being passed to the learning layers.

components.name: Episode Collection Engine
components.description: The core logic for transforming raw external or internal traffic into structured episodes. it manages the internal memory buffer, handles deduplication, and enforces LRU-based eviction policies to maintain system stability. It acts as the primary sink for the system's "Accumulate" phase.

components.name: Server Orchestrator
components.description: The central management authority that coordinates the high-level lifecycle of the agent. It maintains the global state snapshot, manages the transition between learning and idle states, and provides a unified view of the system's performance metrics and playbook insights.

components.name: API & Event Gateway
components.description: Handles all external-facing communication, including secure HTTP ingestion routes and real-time event broadcasting. It ensures that only authorized clients can interact with the hub and provides a reactive Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream for the monitoring dashboard.

components_relations.relation: Routes authenticated ingestion requests (OpenAI-style or internal) to the collector for normalization and buffering.
Updates the global state with new episode metrics and triggers learning cycles when batch thresholds are met.
Pushes state snapshots and system events (e.g., reward trends, learning progress) to the SSE broadcaster for real-time monitoring.

components.name: Feedback & Reward Engine
components.description: Enriches collected episodes with qualitative learning signals. It executes a pipeline of reward extractors that analyze agent actions (e.g., formatting, execution success) and monitors system "intensity" to determine when the collected data warrants a reflection or optimization step.

components.name: Reward Extraction Pipeline
components.description: Processes raw agent episodes through a series of specialized extractors to generate normalized reward signals. It evaluates agent actions based on formatting, tool execution success, and direct user feedback, populating the EpisodeSummary with confidence-weighted values.

components.name: Adaptive Intensity Controller
components.description: Analyzes reward trends to manage the learning loop's cadence. It monitors the variance and magnitude of rewards to detect performance plateaus ("stagnation") and enforces cooldown periods to ensure system stability before triggering a reflection or optimization step.

components_relations.relation: Supplies normalized reward signals and episode summaries used to calculate performance trends and trigger optimization gates

components.name: Active Environment Adapter
components.description: Facilitates active data collection by running agents within controlled task environments (like OpenClaw). Unlike the passive proxy, this component manages the setup and execution of specific tasks, generating complete execution traces that are then fed back into the collection hub.

components.name: Proxy Infrastructure Manager
components.description: Manages the lifecycle of the internal proxy server, including ephemeral port allocation, background thread management for the `uvicorn` server, and configuration of the `ProxyApp` to intercept agent-environment traffic.

components.name: Active Task Runner
components.description: Handles the core execution logic by spawning external processes (typically Node.js scripts) that run the agent. It manages the subprocess lifecycle, pipes task JSON to the runner, and handles error states or timeouts during execution.

components.name: Episode Data Synchronizer
components.description: Bridges the gap between the asynchronous proxy collection and the synchronous task execution loop. It uses thread-safe mechanisms to capture episodes generated by the proxy and signals the runner when data is ready for retrieval.

components.name: Task & Metadata Registry
components.description: Provides the interface for discovering available tasks from environment data sources (e.g., JSONL files) and manages the extraction of session-specific identifiers for export to external observability tools.

components_relations.relation: Configures the interception layer and provides the local proxy URL required for the subprocess environment.

components.name: Optimization & Weight Backend
components.description: Manages "Hard Updates" involving model selection and weight fine‑tuning. It bridges the framework to low‑level RL backends like SkyRL and handles data exportation for observability.

components.name: Learning Orchestrator
components.description: Acts as the primary controller for the learning process. It manages model selection (routing), maintains the state of learning layers, and triggers the forward_backward and optim_step sequences that drive weight updates.

components.name: Routing Decision Engine
components.description: Responsible for real-time model selection by analyzing query features and applying weighted scoring to determine the most cost-effective LLM tier (Light, Medium, Heavy, or Reasoning).

components.name: Adaptive Learning Controller
components.description: Implements the learning protocol (forward/backward/optim) to adapt routing weights and thresholds based on historical performance and cost rewards collected during execution.

components.name: State Identity & Persistence
components.description: Manages the content-addressed identity of the orchestrator's state using SHA-256 hashing and handles the serialization of learned parameters to ensure durability and version tracking across sessions.

components_relations.relation: Supplies routing outcomes and query features to the optimization buffer for future weight updates.
Updates the scoring weights and tier thresholds used for future model classification decisions.
Serializes and restores the routing configuration and learned weights to maintain consistency across restarts.
Generates unique cryptographic identifiers for weight snapshots and manages the lifecycle of the optimization state.

components.name: Weight Optimization Engine
components.description: Implements the "Hard Update" logic by interfacing with specific RL backends. It handles the actual optimization algorithms, weight sampling, and persistence of neural network parameters.

components.name: Backend Orchestrator
components.description: Manages the lifecycle, configuration, and backend‑specific initialization for the weight engine. It validates environment dependencies (Transformers, SkyRL), instantiates the appropriate training backend (e.g., JAX or FSDP), and enforces the ClawLoopBackend protocol.

components.name: Gradient & Advantage Processor
components.description: Implements the "Accumulate" phase of the optimization cycle. It utilizes the SkyRLExporter to convert agent episodes into raw data and performs GRPO advantage computation by grouping rewards and applying group‑mean subtraction to stabilize the RL signal before gradient computation.

components.name: Weight Update & Persistence
components.description: Executes the "Apply" phase of the optimization cycle and manages the long‑term storage of model parameters. It triggers the actual weight updates using Adam‑based optimization and handles the checkpointing of LoRA adapters to ensure learning progress is persisted and recoverable.

components_relations.relation: Provides the initialized backend environment and configuration context required to process episodes into training batches
Supplies the prepared model batches and computed advantages necessary to execute the optimization step
Persists updated adapter states and checkpoint references that are used by the orchestrator during backend re‑initialization or recovery

components.name: Training Data Adapter
components.description: A specialized transformation layer that bridges the gap between high-level framework episodes and the specific data requirements of RL training engines. It handles tokenization and trajectory-to-transition conversion.

components.name: Episode Data Model
components.description: Defines the canonical data structures for agent interactions, capturing the sequence of messages and terminal rewards that constitute a training sample.

components.name: SkyRL Export Engine
components.description: Implements the logic to transform episodes into RL‑ready transitions, including tokenization and chat template application specific to the target model.

components_relations.relation: Consumes episode trajectories and message history to generate tokenized training transitions

components.name: Episode & Observability Core
components.description: Manages the fundamental data structures (Episodes, Messages) and provides the telemetry infrastructure. it tracks rewards and steps, ensuring that interaction data is available for both training and external monitoring via OpenTelemetry.

components.name: Episode Lifecycle & State Management
components.description: Manages the structural definition and lifecycle of agent‑environment interactions. It handles the accumulation of steps, message formatting for LLM compatibility (OpenAI format), and the preservation of metadata like token usage and rewards. It acts as the "source of truth" for any single interaction sequence.

components.name: Telemetry & Performance Analytics
components.description: Transforms raw episode data into high‑level performance metrics and exports them to external observability platforms. It calculates normalized rewards, determines evaluation needs (e.g., via needs_judge), and implements the OpenTelemetry tracing protocol to visualize the agent's decision‑making process.
